description: Complete frozen frame inventory for the_living_seed.py. Updated Session 13.

elements.name: beta
elements.role: Input coupling weight in the core product term
elements.notes: Global coupling. Per-cell decomposition caused 53% MI loss (c006, c007). Permanently blocked until new mechanism proposed addressing c001-c007.

elements.name: gamma
elements.role: Signal modulation weight in the core product term
elements.notes: Global coupling. Same block as beta (c006, c007).

elements.name: eps
elements.role: Inter-cell attention pull strength. Controls lateral communication in state update.
elements.notes: LOCAL parameter — each cell's own pull strength, not a global coupling coefficient. Avoids c006/c007 (confirmed by team-lead ruling Session 13). Task #19 tests binding status. eps=0 → no inter-cell communication. eps=0.5 → strong pull toward neighbors.

elements.name: tau
elements.role: Attention softmax temperature. Controls sharpness of attention weights.
elements.notes: Structural — determines attention focus (diffuse vs winner-take-all). Task #19 tests binding status. Low tau = soft/diffuse attention. High tau = sharp attention.

elements.name: delta
elements.role: State mixing ratio: (1-delta)*x + delta*phi. Memory vs plasticity balance.
elements.notes: Highest priority Stage 4 candidate (Session 13 analysis). Controls memory persistence per cell. delta=0 → state never changes (pure memory). delta=1 → state = pure computation output (no memory). Task #19 tests binding status.

elements.name: noise
elements.role: Gaussian state noise injected at each step
elements.notes: Likely non-binding (scaling analog to alpha clip bounds). Low priority.

elements.name: clip
elements.role: State value hard bound (max absolute state value)
elements.notes: Structural (defines reachable state space). Likely non-binding by analogy with alpha clip bounds (c025). Not yet tested. Lower priority than eps/tau/delta.

elements.name: eta
elements.role: Per-step plasticity learning rate
elements.notes: Stage 3 target. Proven vacuous: system insensitive to learning rate heterogeneity (c011). 7 sessions tested, 5 approaches. Amendment 1 candidate pending Criterion 2 mechanism test.

elements.name: threshold
elements.role: Symmetry-breaking branch threshold: |dev| < threshold triggers random push
elements.notes: Stage 4 candidate — eliminated. Non-binding across [0.01, 0.1] (100x range). 10-seed validation, all p>0.5 (c023). Phase 1 diagnostic using 3 independent seeds showed false +20% (c024).

elements.name: alpha_clip_lo
elements.role: Lower clip bound for alpha
elements.notes: STATUS REVISED (Session 13 multi-K). K=6-only: non-binding (c025). Multi-K: VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3] shows d=7.583, p=0.000 (entry 042). Direction: tighter = better (anti-adaptive). Birth confound pending (Task #23). Even if binding, anti-signal direction means Stage 4 adaptive mechanism cannot improve over optimal fixed value. NOT a Stage 4 adaptive target. c025 PROVISIONALLY SUPERSEDED by c028.

elements.name: alpha_clip_hi
elements.role: Upper clip bound for alpha
elements.notes: STATUS REVISED (Session 13 multi-K). Same revision as ff010. Optimal range appears to be VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3] — reducing both lo and hi. Upper bound at 1.3 vs canonical 1.8. Anti-signal: adaptive expansion harmful. Birth confound (Task #23) will disambiguate whether effect is initialization artifact or adaptation-time dynamics.

elements.name: symmetry_break_mult
elements.role: Magnitude multiplier for symmetry-breaking push
elements.notes: Scaling only — affects push magnitude, not rule structure. Stage 6 candidate (functional form).

elements.name: amplify_mult
elements.role: Magnitude multiplier for diversity-amplification push
elements.notes: Scaling only. Stage 6 candidate.

elements.name: drift_mult
elements.role: Magnitude multiplier for gentle drift push
elements.notes: Scaling only. Stage 6 candidate.

elements.name: attention_plasticity_width
elements.role: Gaussian kernel width in plast = exp(-(fp_d^2)/0.0225). Controls how quickly attention plasticity decays with state change distance.
elements.notes: Never tested. Equivalent to the width of the gating function for inter-cell pull. Could affect how many cells contribute to the pull at any given step.

elements.name: birth_alpha_distribution
elements.role: Initial alpha distribution at birth: Uniform[0.4, 1.8]
elements.notes: Initial condition, not an ongoing parameter. May affect convergence speed but likely not final attractor.

elements.name: resp_z_normalization
elements.role: Response z-scoring formula: z = (response - global_mean) / global_std
elements.notes: The global normalization is frozen. Using per-cell or local normalization would change the adaptation dynamics. Untested, not currently a priority.

elements.name: D
elements.role: State vector dimension per cell
elements.notes: Dimensional parameter. Changing it changes the system fundamentally. Not a per-cell adaptive target.

elements.name: NC
elements.role: Number of cells
elements.notes: The topology (1D ring, NC=6, periodic boundary) is the Stage 5 target. Making connectivity adaptive requires topology change.

elements.name: topology
elements.value: 1D ring over DIMENSIONS (D=12), periodic; fixed all-to-all attention pattern over cells
elements.role: Two distinct topologies: (1) dimensional ring within each cell — kp=(k+1)%D, km=(k-1)%D couples adjacent dimensions in core equation phi[i][k]=tanh(alpha[i][k]*x[i][k]+beta*x[i][kp]*x[i][km]); (2) fixed all-to-all attention pattern over cells — every cell attends to every other cell via dot-product/D*tau, pull gated by plast*eps
elements.notes: Stage 5 target. CORRECTED: Two separate topologies frozen here. (1) Dimensional ring: k indexes dimensions 0..D-1, ring is intra-cell. No inter-cell coupling in core equation. (2) Attention pattern: currently fixed all-to-all (every cell j contributes to every cell i's pull). Stage 5 makes the ATTENTION PATTERN adaptive — which cells attend to which. eps=0 means zero inter-cell communication regardless of attention weights. Per-cell tau/eps (Stage 4 candidates) means per-cell communication parameters, not per-cell topology.

elements.name: equation_form
elements.role: The mathematical form of the core computation
elements.notes: Stage 6 target. The tanh, product coupling structure, and three-branch plasticity rule form are all frozen. Making the functional form adaptive requires parameterizing the equation family.

summary.pending_task22: Task #22 (multi-K revalidation) will determine binding status of ff003/ff004/ff005. Task #19 K=6-only results unreliable (c027).
summary.pending_task23: Task #23 (birth confound) will determine whether ff010/ff011 binding is adaptation-time or initialization artifact (c028)
